macro_summary: Tech and AI capital expenditure narrative dominates the week ahead, with June CPI and Fed Chair Warsh congressional testimony on July 14 serving as the first binary macro gate. The Fed remains split on 2026 rate policy (9/18 officials favor at least one hike), while futures price only ~15% probability of a July move — a soft CPI print would remove the last hike fear and unlock multiple expansion into a dense earnings wave. TSMC reports July 16, kicking off a semiconductor sector re-rating sequence that runs through MSFT (Jul 29), SKHY (Jul 29), AAPL (Jul 30), and NVDA (late August). SOX sits 11% below its June peak, creating a potential mean-reversion runway if earnings confirm AI capex durability.
screening_note: 6 independent overnight screening runs were analyzed. Minimum threshold to qualify: 2 appearances. All 6 qualifying ideas are included, ranked by appearance count descending.
key_events: Jul 13: SKHY ticker begins regular trading (migrates from SKHYV)
Jul 14: June CPI release — binary inflation read for rate trajectory
Jul 14: Fed Chair Warsh congressional testimony — rate clarity event
Jul 16: TSMC Q2 2026 earnings — sector-wide semiconductor re-rating trigger
Jul 29: MSFT Q4 FY2026 earnings + FY2027 AI capex guidance
Jul 29: SKHY Q2 2026 earnings (revenue expected to roughly double QoQ)
Jul 30: AAPL Q3 FY2026 earnings + foldable iPhone cycle buzz
Late Aug: NVDA Q2 FY2027 earnings

ideas.name: NVIDIA Corporation
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 5 of 6 overnight screens with 4 high-conviction votes. NVDA trades at a forward P/E of ~21.7x versus its 5-year average of 72x (Goldman Sachs), a historic valuation trough, while HBM supply remains sold out through most of 2027 and operating margins hold at 64%. TSMC's July 16 earnings serve as the nearest read-through catalyst, followed by the hyperscaler earnings wave (MSFT Jul 29, GOOGL/META/AMZN late Jul) confirming AI capex — all building toward NVDA's own late-August print.
ideas.catalyst: TSMC earnings July 16 (supply chain read-through); hyperscaler AI capex confirmations July 23–31; NVDA Q2 FY2027 earnings late August
ideas.edge: 61 analysts avg 12-mo target $301.62 vs ~$210 current (~43% implied upside); forward P/E 21.7x vs. 5-yr avg 72x; $80B buyback authorized; SOX 11% below June peak creates mean-reversion runway
ideas.bull_case: TSMC beat on Jul 16 re-rates the SOX, hyperscaler capex guidance confirms AI demand durability, and NVDA re-rates toward its historical multiple — 43% upside to consensus target with supply moat intact through 2027.
ideas.bear_case: Hot CPI on Jul 14 reprices rate hike odds, compressing tech multiples further; any hyperscaler capex disappointment hits NVDA disproportionately; stock -17% from May peak shows elevated volatility risk heading into multiple binary events.

ideas.name: Microsoft Corporation
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 5 of 6 overnight screens with a perfect 5 high-conviction votes — the strongest conviction reading in this digest. MSFT trades at its cheapest forward multiple since 2023 (~21-22x NTM) despite 40% Azure growth and a $37B AI run rate, with 97 analysts majority-rated Buy and a consensus target of ~$589-592 versus a current price near $383 (~54% implied upside). The July 29 earnings print will reveal FY2027 AI capex guidance, a direct read-through for the entire AI infrastructure trade.
ideas.catalyst: Q4 FY2026 earnings July 29, 2026 — will provide FY2027 AI capex guidance; June CPI July 14 as macro pre-condition
ideas.edge: Consensus analyst target ~$589-$592 vs. ~$383 current (~54% implied upside); cheapest forward multiple (21-22x NTM) since 2023; Q3 revenue +17.9% YoY; Azure AI re-acceleration is the unlock
ideas.bull_case: Azure growth re-accelerates and FY2027 capex guidance impresses — stock re-rates toward consensus $590 target, simultaneously validating the broader AI capex trade and lifting NVDA/SKHY.
ideas.bear_case: A hot July 14 CPI print or hawkish Warsh testimony compresses valuations ahead of earnings; any Azure deceleration or cautious capex guidance could extend the -25% YTD drawdown and weigh on AI sector broadly.

ideas.name: Apple Inc.
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 5 of 6 overnight screens. AAPL has surged 8%+ in July alone heading into a July 30 earnings print with a guidance range of 14-17% revenue growth, an 8-quarter consecutive EPS beat streak, and foldable iPhone cycle buzz for fall. Services revenue hit an all-time high of $31B/quarter and iPhone sales jumped 22% in fiscal Q2, placing AAPL within 4% of overtaking NVDA as the world's most valuable company. All three screening runs preferred defined-risk call spreads over outright stock given the 37x P/E premium.
ideas.catalyst: Fiscal Q3 FY2026 earnings July 30, 2026; foldable iPhone lineup rumored for fall debut generating pre-earnings buzz
ideas.edge: 8 consecutive EPS beats; 14-17% revenue growth guidance; Services at all-time high $31B/quarter; $100B buyback active; stock up 8%+ in July vs. flat first half — momentum and catalyst aligned
ideas.bull_case: Strong iPhone and Services beat plus foldable iPhone product excitement drives a 9th consecutive EPS beat, sustaining momentum toward NVDA's market cap; buyback provides floor.
ideas.bear_case: 37x P/E leaves little room for error; China/tariff risk remains an unresolved overhang; if pre-earnings rally has already priced in the beat, post-earnings fade is a real risk even on inline results.

ideas.name: SK Hynix Inc. (ADR)
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 5 of 6 overnight screens. SKHY begins regular trading today (ticker migrates from SKHYV on July 13) as the largest foreign company US listing ever ($26.5B), following an IPO priced at $149 that closed day-1 at ~$168 (+13%) on 7x oversubscribed demand. SK Hynix dominates HBM memory supply — sold out through most of 2027 — with revenue +41% YoY and net income +108% YoY. All 5 runs preferred stock entry over options given options market immaturity in the first week of regular trading.
ideas.catalyst: Permanent ticker SKHY begins regular trading July 13; Q2 2026 earnings July 29 expected to show revenue roughly doubling QoQ; TSMC earnings July 16 as sector re-rating event; potential US index eligibility pipeline
ideas.edge: IPO 7x oversubscribed; 35 of 37 covering analysts rate Buy; EPS +104% YoY; HBM supply sold out through 2027; valuation discount to US-listed peer Micron not yet closed; largest foreign US listing ever signals structural institutional demand
ideas.bull_case: TSMC July 16 beat re-rates the HBM/memory complex, SKHY's July 29 earnings confirm the revenue doubling, and index inclusion pipeline drives forced buying — discount to Micron closes rapidly.
ideas.bear_case: Post-IPO lock-up and stabilization dynamics may create early volatility; options market illiquidity limits hedging; any macro shock (hot CPI, rate hike) hits high-multiple growth names hardest in the first weeks of trading.

ideas.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 2 of 6 overnight screens, both with high conviction. TSMC reports Q2 2026 earnings on July 16 — just 5 days away — with Polymarket assigning 94.5% probability of a beat, Q2 revenue guided to $39-40.2B (+32% YoY), and monthly revenue filings already de-risking the print. A beat would simultaneously re-rate NVDA, SKHY, and ASML as a sector-wide catalyst, with the SOX sitting 11% below its June peak as the compression entry point. Both runs preferred defined-risk bull call spreads expiring end of July.
ideas.catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings July 16, 2026 — 5 days away; Citi placed a 30-day upside catalyst watch pre-earnings
ideas.edge: Avg analyst target ~$520 implies ~17-19% upside from ~$432; consensus Strong Buy (17 buys, 2 holds, 0 sells); 94.5% Polymarket beat probability; Q2 semi industry earnings projected +131% YoY (FactSet); SOX 11% below June peak
ideas.bull_case: TSMC beats and raises on July 16 with AI-driven demand commentary, triggering a SOX mean-reversion rally and validating the AI capex read-through for NVDA, SKHY, and the broader hyperscaler trade simultaneously.
ideas.bear_case: Taiwan geopolitical risk is a permanent overhang; even a strong print may be sell-the-news if pre-earnings momentum has run; post-selloff vol elevation raises option premium cost.

ideas.name: Invesco QQQ Trust
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 2 of 6 overnight screens, both medium conviction, with divergent directional views — one bullish (CPI soft = unlock for tech multiples) and one bearish hedge (hot CPI = hike repricing with record margin debt amplifying downside). The July 14 CPI + Warsh testimony binary is 3 days away, making this a near-term event-driven macro play rather than a directional conviction. Both runs agreed defined-risk options spreads are the appropriate vehicle — bull call spreads for the soft-CPI scenario, put spreads as a portfolio hedge for the hot-CPI tail.
ideas.catalyst: June CPI release July 14, 2026 + Fed Chair Warsh congressional testimony July 14; FOMC meeting July 28-29
ideas.edge: Rates market pricing only ~15% probability of July hike (Kalshi); weak June jobs (57K vs 115K expected) tilts toward hold; a soft CPI removes last hike fear ahead of dense earnings week; put spreads offer cheap hedge given record margin debt ($1.42T, +54% YoY) amplifying any downside
ideas.bull_case: Soft June CPI + Warsh hold signal removes last rate hike fear, unlocking tech multiple expansion heading into MSFT/AAPL earnings — QQQ bull call spread captures upside with defined risk.
ideas.bear_case: Hot CPI reprices Fed hike probability materially; with margin debt at record $1.42T (+54% YoY) and Nasdaq up ~11% YTD, a hawkish macro surprise could compress multiples sharply — put spreads provide portfolio hedge at low cost relative to complacency.
